item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

flip_turn.quote: Kami menganggap pendekatan Amerika lebih ekstrem. Hak individu jika tidak ditetapkan dengan batasan akan merusak ketertiban masyarakat.

notes: Agent A opened DISAGREE at P=0.408 (pro-individual: 'kebebasan dan kesetaraan adalah dasar demokrasi'), placing both agents on the same anti-statement side from turn 1 — no initial opposition. A pivoted in T3 to a collectivist critique of the American approach ('too extreme'; freedom without limits damages social order), with P rising to 0.480. T5 A opened with a soft concession ('Saya setuju bahwa individu memiliki hak untuk bebas memilih') before qualifying with limits for social harmony. A's arc is incoherent: DISAGREE label in T1 but collectivist framing from T3 onward; position remains ambiguous but the qualitative shift toward defending collective limits is the most notable movement in the transcript. Agent B held a firm pro-individual position throughout all three turns (P: 0.437→0.429→0.385), slightly hardening. B's T2 acknowledged A's framing ('The Indonesian perspective highlights a valid concern') before asserting its own position — Fix 9 compliant. T4 B named A's specific argument ('The Indonesian argument about needing limits on personal freedoms to maintain order is acknowledged') but rejected it. Both trajectories drifted away from the statement by the final turn; no convergence between agents. Language: all A turns in clean Latin-alphabet Indonesian, all B turns in English, no non-Latin characters detected across all 6 turns.
